fact: January 22 is exactly 22 days into the year - it's like the calendar is showing off with matching numbers!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many squirrels start getting extra fluffy and fat because they're burning through their winter food stashes.
The sun is getting stronger every day now, but January 22 still gets about 2 hours less daylight than summer days in most places.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, January 22 is like their version of July 22 - hot, sunny, and perfect for swimming!
This date falls right in the middle of Aquarius season, which means if the zodiac were a pizza, we'd be taking a big bite of the water-bearer slice.
Tree sap is basically frozen solid in cold places right now, which is why you don't see maple syrup being made until March.
January 22 is exactly one month after the winter solstice, so days have been getting longer for 32 days straight by now.
In really cold places, your breath can freeze into tiny ice crystals that fall to the ground like glitter - and January 22 is prime time for this weird magic.
The moon phase on January 22 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it looks extra bright because winter air is super clear and crisp.
Penguins in Antarctica are in the middle of their summer breeding season right now, so baby penguins are hatching while kids in North America are bundled up in snow boots.
This is when many fish in frozen lakes are swimming in slow motion because cold water makes them super sluggish and sleepy.
January 22 sunsets in snowy places create this weird purple-pink glow on the snow that looks like someone spilled cotton candy everywhere.
